title: kube play build should support nested directories
body:
### Feature request description

When playing with the `podman kube play --build` command, we may want to provide our full project as context-dir. However, the image name to build does not support nested directories.

```
.
└── my-project/
    ├── mocks/
    │   ├── service-a/
    │   │   └── Containerfile
    │   ├── service-b/
    │   │   └── Containerfile
    │   └── play.yaml
    ├── packages/
    │   ├── foo/
    │   └── bar/
    └── shared.config.json
```

Let's take an example, I've got some services in `mocks/*` and a `mocks/play.yaml`, however, if I specify `image: service-a` it will not found it if context-dir is the full project.

If I want to build `service-a` and `service-b` I need the `context-dir` to be in `mocks`, but if the Containerfile need more context (for example the `shared.config.json`) or the `packages/*` this is impossible.

### Suggest potential solution

I can see two solutions

1. Search in more location

When searching for the `<name>.Containerfile`, it searches from the context-dir root, we could do from the context-dir root, and the yaml file.

2. Support nested path

For example `mocks/service-a:latest` should search for `service-a` first (backward compatibility) and `mocks/service-a` second etc.

This would be easy, as the logic to extract path is located in [pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L1632](
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4c3027c149ad54dbb6f96328694e7b852623b4b8/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L1632)

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4c3027c149ad54dbb6f96328694e7b852623b4b8/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L1632

We could instead of returning a single string, return an array of path to search for.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

N/A

### Additional context

I'm open to work on this issue

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
